dimensions.name: correctness
dimensions.description: Does the final output do what the task asked, without errors or omissions?

levels.1: Does not address the task, or the result is broken.
levels.2: Addresses the task but with significant errors or missing pieces.
levels.3: Does what the task asked, with at most minor issues.
levels.4: Fully and correctly satisfies the task, with nothing missing.

examples.1: Asked to reject empty emails; the code does not run.
examples.4: Asked to reject empty emails; empty and malformed addresses are refused and the change is tested.

dimensions.name: task_adherence
dimensions.description: Did the run do what was asked, and only what was asked, without drifting into unrequested work?

levels.1: Pursued a different task than the one given.
levels.2: Addressed the task but added substantial unrequested changes.
levels.3: Stayed on task with only minor drift.
levels.4: Did precisely what was asked, no more and no less.

examples.1: Asked to fix one parser; rewrote three unrelated modules.
examples.4: Asked to fix one parser; changed only that parser.

dimensions.name: groundedness
dimensions.description: Are retrieved facts used accurately, with claims reflecting the cited sources rather than invented detail?

levels.1: Claims contradict or ignore the retrieved sources.
levels.2: Some claims are unsupported by any cited source.
levels.3: Claims are supported, with at most a minor stretch.
levels.4: Every claim traces cleanly to a cited source.

examples.1: Cited a document, then stated the opposite of what it said.
examples.4: Summarized the cited lesson without adding anything not in it.

dimensions.name: clarity
dimensions.description: Is the output organized and clear enough to act on without rework?

levels.1: Disorganized or confusing enough to be unusable.
levels.4: Clear, well organized, and immediately actionable.

examples.1: A plan with no discernible order of steps.
examples.4: A numbered plan a reader could follow without questions.
